description:
Advances: specled.evidence_store, specled.mix_tasks

Deliverable:
1. SpecLedEx.Evidence.Sync (lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex): fetch remote ref (`git fetch origin +refs/heads/spec-evidence:refs/remotes/origin/spec-evidence`) -> per-key tree-union merge via plumbing ONLY (union of entry paths from local and remote evidence trees; same-path/different-blob resolved by reading both entries' stamps and keeping max — identical rule to S2 local replace) -> assemble merged tree under a unique GIT_INDEX_FILE -> commit-tree (parents: local tip + remote tip when both exist; unrelated self-created roots merge identically — reconciliation is content-based, never history-based) -> update local ref -> push `--force-with-lease=refs/heads/spec-evidence:<fetched-sha>`; on lease rejection refetch -> re-merge -> retry: 3 attempts, 250ms x attempt jittered. Remote-absent -> plain push; local-absent -> adopt remote. Orphan ref never checked out.
2. mix spec.sync (lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex): default mode raises (Mix.raise, non-zero) on persistent failure; --best-effort flag emits exactly one well-formed warning and exits 0 (the hook's mode). Output surfaces ahead/behind entry counts labeled "as of last fetch" (drift surfacing Should-Have).
3. mix spec.prune (lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex): fetch, compute live tree-hash set (`git log --format=%T` over local branch heads + remote-tracking refs), drop unreachable entries, commit + CAS-push via the same Sync path. Explicit-invocation only; moduledoc warns against pruning during active review windows.
4. Spec amendments: evidence_store.spec.md — flip the sync-group verification stub (sync_tree_union, sync_failure_contracts, prune_explicit_only, drift_surfaced) execute:false->true as tests land; mix_tasks.spec.md gains the two new tasks.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: new test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs. Flip evidence_store sync-group stub.
- Behaviors to demonstrate (scenario IDs from evidence_store.spec.md): scenario.sync_converges_in_both_push_orders (two clones of one bare origin; disjoint-entry union; same-key conflict resolved by constructed stamps in BOTH push orders — order-independence asserted; unrelated self-created roots converge; lease rejection -> refetch-retry succeeds; no worktree ever checks out spec-evidence); scenario.sync_exhaustion_modes (default raises, --best-effort exactly one warning + exit 0); scenario.prune_drops_only_unreachable (reachable via local head kept, via remote-tracking kept, unreachable dropped; plain sync never pruned).
- Verification cadence:
  - Iteration tier: compile-graph-scoped checks only (mix test --stale or named files) plus mix spec.check --no-run-commands.
  - Final tier: full merge-gate suite run-until-green before IMPLEMENTED.
  - Verifier tier: flipped stubs + targeted/--stale only; no full suite.
- Standing bounce-class checks:
  - Production call site named for each new callable surface, with proving test on that production path
  - Fixture hygiene: bare-origin + two-clone fixture repos torn down; no cross-test leakage
  - Spec surfaces fresh for every touched callable/config surface
  - Every conditional clause covered (remote-absent, local-absent, lease-reject, exhaustion, both merge parent shapes)
- Call-site Integration: Evidence.Sync.run/2 called from Mix.Tasks.Spec.Sync.run/1 (and later the S5 hook shim); proven by spec_sync_task_test.exs driving the task against a bare-origin + two-clone fixture through the real push/lease path.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/{tree_hash,entry,store}.ex (S2, done — read-only deps)
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex (S2 done; S5 changes it)
- lib/specled_ex/review/** (S4)
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.evidence.*, priv/hooks/** (S5)
- docs/**, priv/spec_init/**, skills/**, .gitignore
Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex
- test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs
- test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs
- test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs
- .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md (stub flips execute:false->true; amendments only via ADR)
- .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md (two new tasks + stub flips)
- .spec/state.json, .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-managed regen — NOT scope creep)

Out of Scope (intent):
- spec.check stays zero-network — do NOT auto-invoke sync from check (pinned).
- No hook installation (S5); spec.sync is hand-invocable from this stage on.
- No prune-on-sync — sync must never delete entries.
- No history-based merge logic anywhere; content/tree-union only.
- Escalation valve: if --force-with-lease semantics against non-tracking refs surface a genuine unknown on the CI git version, ESCALATE (mega-plan trigger), do not work around in place.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (mix spec.check; adjudicate against S1 baseline note)
- [ ] mix format --check-formatted; mix deps.unlock --check-unused
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket
title: S3: Sync machinery: spec.sync + spec.prune + drift surfacing
